# Test
dn = DiracNotation()

ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]])
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]])

print("\nInner products:")

print(f"⟨0|1⟩ = {dn.inner_product(ket_0, ket_1)}")

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class DiracNotation:
    """Tools for working with Dirac notation."""
    
    @staticmethod
    def ket_to_bra(ket: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Convert |ψ⟩ to ⟨ψ|."""
        return ket.conj().T
    
    @staticmethod
    def inner_product(state1: np.ndarray, state2: np.ndarray) -> complex:
        """Compute ⟨state1|state2⟩."""
        bra1 = state1.conj().T
        return (bra1 @ state2)[0, 0]
    
    @staticmethod
    def outer_product(ket1: np.ndarray, ket2: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Compute |ket1⟩⟨ket2|."""
        bra2 = ket2.conj().T
        return ket1 @ bra2
    
    @staticmethod
    def projector(state: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Create projector |ψ⟩⟨ψ|."""
        return DiracNotation.outer_product(state, state)
    
    @staticmethod
    def is_projector(matrix: np.ndarray) -> bool:
        """Check if matrix is a valid projector (P² = P, P† = P)."""
        # Check idempotent: P² = P
        idempotent = np.allclose(matrix @ matrix, matrix)
        # Check Hermitian: P† = P
        hermitian = np.allclose(matrix.conj().T, matrix)
        return idempotent and hermitian

# Test
dn = DiracNotation()

ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]])
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]])
ket_psi = np.array([[1], [1j]]) / np.sqrt(2)

print("Bra-ket conversions:")
print(f"⟨0| = {dn.ket_to_bra(ket_0)}")
print(f"⟨ψ| = {dn.ket_to_bra(ket_psi)}")

print("\nInner products:")
print(f"⟨0|0⟩ = {dn.inner_product(ket_0, ket_0)}")
print(f"⟨0|1⟩ = {dn.inner_product(ket_0, ket_1)}")
print(f"⟨ψ|ψ⟩ = {dn.inner_product(ket_psi, ket_psi)}")

print("\nOuter products:")
print(f"|0⟩⟨1| =\n{dn.outer_product(ket_0, ket_1)}")

print("\nProjector:")
P = dn.projector(ket_psi)
print(f"|ψ⟩⟨ψ| =\n{P}")
print(f"Is projector? {dn.is_projector(P)}")
hints: Projector satisfies P² = P and P† = P

exercises.title: Operator Builder
exercises.description: Build quantum operators from their Dirac notation representations.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def build_operator(terms: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Build an operator from a list of (coefficient, ket1, ket2) terms.
    Each term represents coeff * |ket1⟩⟨ket2|
    
    Args:
        terms: List of (coefficient, ket1, ket2) tuples
    
    Returns:
        The resulting operator as a matrix
    """

    pass

def verify_gate(operator: np.ndarray, name: str) -> dict:
    """
    Verify properties of a quantum gate.
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with verification results
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Define basis states
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]])
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]])
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]]) / np.sqrt(2)
ket_minus = np.array([[1], [-1]]) / np.sqrt(2)

# Build Pauli gates
print("Building X = |0⟩⟨1| + |1⟩⟨0|:")
X = build_operator([(1, ket_0, ket_1), (1, ket_1, ket_0)])
print(X)
print(verify_gate(X, "X"))

print("\nBuilding Z = |0⟩⟨0| - |1⟩⟨1|:")
Z = build_operator([(1, ket_0, ket_0), (-1, ket_1, ket_1)])
print(Z)

print("\nBuilding H = |+⟩⟨0| + |-⟩⟨1|:")
H = build_operator([(1, ket_plus, ket_0), (1, ket_minus, ket_1)])
print(np.round(H, 4))
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def build_operator(terms: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Build an operator from a list of (coefficient, ket1, ket2) terms.
    """
    if not terms:
        return None
    
    # Get dimension from first term
    dim = terms[0][1].shape[0]
    operator = np.zeros((dim, dim), dtype=complex)
    
    for coeff, ket1, ket2 in terms:
        # Add coeff * |ket1⟩⟨ket2|
        outer = ket1 @ ket2.conj().T
        operator += coeff * outer
    
    return operator

def verify_gate(operator: np.ndarray, name: str) -> dict:
    """
    Verify properties of a quantum gate.
    """
    results = {'name': name}
    
    # Check if unitary: U†U = I
    identity = np.eye(operator.shape[0])
    is_unitary = np.allclose(operator.conj().T @ operator, identity)
    results['is_unitary'] = is_unitary
    
    # Check if Hermitian: A† = A
    is_hermitian = np.allclose(operator.conj().T, operator)
    results['is_hermitian'] = is_hermitian
    
    # Calculate eigenvalues
    eigenvalues = np.linalg.eigvals(operator)
    results['eigenvalues'] = np.round(eigenvalues, 4)
    
    # Check if self-inverse: A² = I
    is_self_inverse = np.allclose(operator @ operator, identity)
    results['is_self_inverse'] = is_self_inverse
    
    return results

# Define basis states
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]])
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]])
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]]) / np.sqrt(2)
ket_minus = np.array([[1], [-1]]) / np.sqrt(2)

# Build Pauli gates
print("Building X = |0⟩⟨1| + |1⟩⟨0|:")
X = build_operator([(1, ket_0, ket_1), (1, ket_1, ket_0)])
print(X)
print(verify_gate(X, "X"))

print("\nBuilding Z = |0⟩⟨0| - |1⟩⟨1|:")
Z = build_operator([(1, ket_0, ket_0), (-1, ket_1, ket_1)])
print(Z)
print(verify_gate(Z, "Z"))

print("\nBuilding H = |+⟩⟨0| + |-⟩⟨1|:")
H = build_operator([(1, ket_plus, ket_0), (1, ket_minus, ket_1)])
print(np.round(H, 4))
print(verify_gate(H, "H"))

print("\nBuilding Y = i|1⟩⟨0| - i|0⟩⟨1|:")
Y = build_operator([(1j, ket_1, ket_0), (-1j, ket_0, ket_1)])
print(Y)
print(verify_gate(Y, "Y"))
hints: Each term is coeff × |ket1⟩⟨ket2| = coeff × (ket1 @ ket2†)
Sum all terms to get the full operator

exercises.title: Spectral Decomposition
exercises.description: Implement spectral decomposition and reconstruct operators from eigenvalues/eigenvectors.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

        operator: Hermitian matrix

    """
    Reconstruct operator from spectral decomposition.
    A = Σᵢ λᵢ |aᵢ⟩⟨aᵢ|
    """

# Test with Pauli matrices
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])

print("Spectral decomposition of X:")
result_X = spectral_decomposition(X)
print(f"Eigenvalues: {result_X['eigenvalues']}")
print(f"Eigenvectors: {[v.flatten() for v in result_X['eigenvectors']]}")
print(f"Dirac form: {result_X['dirac_form']}")

print("\nSpectral decomposition of Z:")
result_Z = spectral_decomposition(Z)
print(f"Eigenvalues: {result_Z['eigenvalues']}")
print(f"Dirac form: {result_Z['dirac_form']}")

# Verify reconstruction
print("\nReconstruction verification:")
X_reconstructed = reconstruct_operator(result_X['eigenvalues'], 
                                        result_X['eigenvectors'])
print(f"X matches reconstruction: {np.allclose(X, X_reconstructed)}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def spectral_decomposition(operator: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """
    Compute the spectral decomposition of a Hermitian operator.
    """
    # Get eigenvalues and eigenvectors
    eigenvalues, eigenvectors = np.linalg.eigh(operator)
    
    # Convert eigenvectors to list of column vectors (kets)
    n = operator.shape[0]
    kets = [eigenvectors[:, i:i+1] for i in range(n)]
    
    # Create projectors
    projectors = [ket @ ket.conj().T for ket in kets]
    
    # Create Dirac notation string
    dirac_terms = []
    for i, (val, ket) in enumerate(zip(eigenvalues, kets)):
        if np.isclose(val, 1):
            coeff = "+"
        elif np.isclose(val, -1):
            coeff = "-"
        else:
            coeff = f"{val:+.2f}"
        dirac_terms.append(f"{coeff}|e{i}⟩⟨e{i}|")
    dirac_form = " ".join(dirac_terms)
    
    return {
        'eigenvalues': eigenvalues,
        'eigenvectors': kets,
        'projectors': projectors,
        'dirac_form': dirac_form
    }

def reconstruct_operator(eigenvalues: list, eigenvectors: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Reconstruct operator from spectral decomposition.
    """
    n = eigenvectors[0].shape[0]
    operator = np.zeros((n, n), dtype=complex)
    
    for val, ket in zip(eigenvalues, eigenvectors):
        projector = ket @ ket.conj().T
        operator += val * projector
    
    return operator

# Test with Pauli matrices
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)

print("Spectral decomposition of X:")
result_X = spectral_decomposition(X)
print(f"Eigenvalues: {result_X['eigenvalues']}")
print(f"Eigenvectors: {[np.round(v.flatten(), 4) for v in result_X['eigenvectors']]}")
print(f"Dirac form: {result_X['dirac_form']}")

print("\nSpectral decomposition of Z:")
result_Z = spectral_decomposition(Z)
print(f"Eigenvalues: {result_Z['eigenvalues']}")
print(f"Eigenvectors: {[np.round(v.flatten(), 4) for v in result_Z['eigenvectors']]}")
print(f"Dirac form: {result_Z['dirac_form']}")

print("\nSpectral decomposition of Y:")
result_Y = spectral_decomposition(Y)
print(f"Eigenvalues: {result_Y['eigenvalues']}")

# Verify reconstruction
print("\nReconstruction verification:")
for name, op, result in [('X', X, result_X), ('Y', Y, result_Y), ('Z', Z, result_Z)]:
    reconstructed = reconstruct_operator(result['eigenvalues'], result['eigenvectors'])
    matches = np.allclose(op, reconstructed)
    print(f"{name} matches reconstruction: {matches}")
hints: Use np.linalg.eigh for Hermitian matrices (returns real eigenvalues)
Eigenvectors come as columns, convert to kets

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

class QuantumMeasurement:
    """
    Simulate quantum measurements using projectors.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, basis_states: list, labels: list = None):
        self.basis = basis_states
        self.labels = labels if labels else list(range(len(basis_states)))
        # Build projectors: Pᵢ = |eᵢ⟩⟨eᵢ|
        self.projectors = [state @ state.conj().T for state in basis_states]
    
    def probabilities(self, state: np.ndarray) -> dict:
        """
        Calculate measurement probabilities for a state.
        """
        probs = {}
        for label, proj in zip(self.labels, self.projectors):
            # P(i) = ⟨ψ|Pᵢ|ψ⟩
            prob = np.real((state.conj().T @ proj @ state)[0, 0])
            probs[label] = prob
        return probs
    
    def measure(self, state: np.ndarray) -> tuple:
        """
        Simulate a measurement, returning (outcome, post_state).
        """
        probs = self.probabilities(state)
        prob_values = list(probs.values())
        
        # Sample outcome
        outcome_idx = np.random.choice(len(self.labels), p=prob_values)
        outcome = self.labels[outcome_idx]
        
        # Post-measurement state: Pᵢ|ψ⟩ / ||Pᵢ|ψ⟩||
        proj = self.projectors[outcome_idx]
        post_state = proj @ state
        norm = np.linalg.norm(post_state)
        post_state = post_state / norm
        
        return outcome, post_state
    
    def expectation_value(self, state: np.ndarray, observable: np.ndarray) -> float:
        """
        Calculate ⟨ψ|A|ψ⟩ for observable A.
        """
        result = state.conj().T @ observable @ state
        return np.real(result[0, 0])

# Define bases
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]])
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]])
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]]) / np.sqrt(2)
ket_minus = np.array([[1], [-1]]) / np.sqrt(2)

# Test computational basis measurement
np.random.seed(42)
comp_measurement = QuantumMeasurement([ket_0, ket_1], ['|0⟩', '|1⟩'])

# Test state: |+⟩
print("Measuring |+⟩ in computational basis:")
probs = comp_measurement.probabilities(ket_plus)
print(f"Probabilities: {probs}")

print("\nSimulating 10 measurements:")
for _ in range(10):
    outcome, post_state = comp_measurement.measure(ket_plus)
    print(f"  Outcome: {outcome}, Post-state: {np.round(post_state.flatten(), 4)}")

# Expectation value
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
print(f"\n⟨+|Z|+⟩ = {comp_measurement.expectation_value(ket_plus, Z)}")
print(f"⟨+|X|+⟩ = {comp_measurement.expectation_value(ket_plus, X)}")
print(f"⟨0|Z|0⟩ = {comp_measurement.expectation_value(ket_0, Z)}")

# Test Hadamard basis measurement
print("\n--- Hadamard basis measurement ---")
had_measurement = QuantumMeasurement([ket_plus, ket_minus], ['|+⟩', '|−⟩'])
print(f"Measuring |0⟩ in Hadamard basis:")
print(f"Probabilities: {had_measurement.probabilities(ket_0)}")
